review-details_detailsLink__NxIv5: The reality of the R&D Department
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9:
If you want a robotic life where you clock in at 8 and clock out at 5:30 and are ok with having no work allocated to you. Congratulations you have hit the jackpot. 

If they know you are an average employee they won't even bother assigning work to you. 

Leisure activities like badminton and dance classes all present in the recreational center. 

You can have decent lunch for 50rs or less in their mess.

This is THE place to work if you are considering chilling and enjoying perks. And yes if you are from the boomer generation this is the culture you were striving for as it is exactly what industrial revolution was trying to achieve.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
Now let's get to the real deal here. 

1. If you want career growth, perhaps you are one of those people who strive and work hard. Who take their work seriously. 2 things are going to happen to you here. Either you are going to beg your manager for work and he still wont give you anything. OR he will assign the MOST CRITICAL work to you and expect you to do it from start to finish. This reeks of bad management practices.

Please note the following points extremely carefully before joining this company:

1. Office timings from 8 to 5:30. It is extremely strict and even though there is flexi policy you need to cover 9.5h per day. 

2. NO i repeat NO project management team or project management tools in the company. They'll slap work on you as soon as it is critical and it may still not be a part of the actual product or you may spend days without work here.

3. People have gone days to years without work in this company. You will rip your hair out in frustration and your manager still won't assign work to you. He or she may only assign it to their favourite employee and overburden that employee only.

4. THERE IS NO WORK FROM HOME POLICY. Please don't listen to the HR when they say that you still might be able to get it. Absolutely not.

5. Holidays are bare minimum. Your manager won't appreciate you applying for leave here. 

6. You wont even get a laptop to work here. An old school computer is all you get. The lab is located far from the work desk or cubicle.

7. They have primitive version management tools in the company.

8. Appraisals.. ah.. here we go. You cannot fight for compensation here. They won't even let you speak for yourself for appraisals. Everything depends on your manager. If you have a good equation with the manager you might get a decent one. Unfair performance ratings are common here which may have nothing to do with your performance.

9. Politics is at it's peak. You are nothing without your manager. Even if he or she doesn't know anything compared to you. You still need to respect him or her. Because they have you in their clutches. A very primitive culture where you absolutely have 0 autonomy when it comes to work. He/She may even threaten to give a bad rating if you dare to do anything other than what they want. They may even feel free to comment on your personal life.

10. This is for the women. This company will rant about being female friendly and everything. But it is not. The company enforces rules like no late nights for women rather than ensuring their safety within the campus. So much for equal opportunity. There have also been cases where work is allocated preferably to a male employee over female as they won't be able to stay late in the office.

I will absolutely not recommend this company as there is no culture and old school practices are still being followed.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: Strong Market Position: Bajaj Auto has established a strong market presence and brand recognition in India and various international markets. The company has a significant market share in the two-wheeler segment, known for its motorcycles and three-wheelers. Its long-standing presence and market leadership contribute to its competitive advantage. Diverse Product Portfolio: Bajaj Auto offers a diverse range of products catering to different customer segments. They manufacture motorcycles, scooters, and three-wheelers, providing options for both personal transportation and commercial use. This diverse product portfolio allows Bajaj Auto to target a wide range of customers and adapt to changing market trends. Technological Innovation: Bajaj Auto emphasizes technological innovation in its product development. The company has a strong focus on research and development (R&D) activities, leading to the introduction of advanced features and technologies in their vehicles. Bajaj Auto's commitment to innovation helps it stay ahead of the competition and meet evolving customer demands. Strong Financial Performance: Bajaj Auto has a track record of delivering strong financial performance. The company has consistently reported healthy revenue growth and profitability. Its robust financial position allows Bajaj Auto to invest in expansion, R&D, and marketing initiatives, enabling future growth and sustainability. Global Presence: Bajaj Auto has successfully expanded its operations beyond India and has a significant presence in several international markets. The company exports its vehicles to various countries across the globe, leveraging its strong brand and product quality. This global footprint enhances revenue diversification and reduces dependence on a single market, mitigating risks associated with a regional economic downturn.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2: Vulnerability to Economic Factors: Bajaj Auto's performance is closely tied to the overall economic conditions, both in India and globally. Economic downturns, fluctuations in exchange rates, and changes in consumer purchasing power can impact the demand for vehicles, affecting the company's sales and profitability. Intense Competition: The automobile industry is highly competitive, and Bajaj Auto faces competition from both domestic and international players. Rival companies constantly introduce new models, pricing strategies, and marketing campaigns, which can put pressure on Bajaj Auto's market share and profitability. Dependence on Two-Wheeler Segment: While Bajaj Auto is a leading player in the two-wheeler market, the company's revenue heavily relies on this segment. Any significant decline in the demand for motorcycles or scooters, or a shift in consumer preferences towards other forms of transportation, could impact Bajaj Auto's financial performance. Regulatory Environment: The automotive industry is subject to various regulations and policies related to emissions, safety standards, and other compliance requirements. Changes in regulations or the introduction of new policies can impose additional costs and operational challenges for Bajaj Auto, potentially affecting its profitability. Product Concentration Risk: Bajaj Auto's product portfolio is focused on motorcycles, scooters, and three-wheelers. While this diversification within the two-wheeler segment is beneficial, the company's reliance on these specific product categories poses a risk. A decline in demand for any of these segments due to changing consumer preferences or market dynamics could have a significant impact on Bajaj Auto's business.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: The company is one of the leading automobile company of India and also most paying company in this sector. As the title says it's a very good company to start your career with but not that good to settle in. You get the freedom to do whatever you want in a project. Your rapo with your seniors defines the kind of freedom at the work you get. The best thing about joining Bajaj as a GTE is the GTE hostel. For almost free of cost you get to live your college hostel life again.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2: As being a 'Marwadi' company, the company is very stringent when it comes to giving out holidays and a job related social life does not exists. The company works on a appraisal system which is sometimes highly partial depending on the relations wit the senior rather than the work done. Again, because of the appraisal system, the inter-department coordination is very weak as every department is in the race to get to the top instead of working together. This is one of the reasons for the project delays. The working style in Bajaj Auto is very orthodox and 'Boss is always right!' goes right along with it.

review-details_detailsLink__NxIv5: Graduate Trainee Engineer at Bajaj Auto
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9:
1. One gets a very good understanding of mechanical engineering application in the automobile industry, can work in core mechanical domain.
2. You get used to handling high pressure and extreme workload.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
1. Very very bad company culture. You'll rarely get an appreciation for your work. But will surely hear insult when the work is not done properly.
2. High workload. 6 days working and many times you have to come on Sundays. Managers over there insist to do 2 hrs extra every day even to newcomers. Frustration is the usual thing.
3.  No value to the employee.  When I was leaving the job, nobody asked me the reason and there was no exit interview (although I worked restlessly for one year for meeting my manager's expectations).

review-details_detailsLink__NxIv5: A good opportunity to learn and grow, professionally.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: Bajaj Auto is a company where there is plenty of opportunity to learn and grow. You are given freedom and good exposure in the company. Performance evaluation is transparent & if you strive hard you definitely get rewarded.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
Being a private ltd. enterprise, the competition is at your tail all the time.
Too much of lean management and cost-cutting going on.
TPM seems a mere formal nuisance at times.
Very few working procedures, rules/changes happen for good, for make things more stringent and complicated.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: Good compensation, freedom to pursue research and development work and try out your ideas. Good location. Decent number of off days for a private company. I had a pretty good boss, so work culture depends on boss and your group but in general I would say it is fairly good. Their R&D has some very good engineers and very cooperative technicians.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2: You overwork sometimes. Most design work on outdated technology, and sometimes slightly difficult to change things. I saw that senior employees worked a lot so it felt as if they didn't have a good work-life balance. Vendor base mostly of poor quality, mostly to keep costs lower, but will become difficult to deal with some vendors, but it is going to happen sometime in your career.

B. You have to request your BOSS, if he really feels like doing it and that's your lucky day, then he will REQUEST to the concerned person (who is manager or higher). Then later it's your luck.
4. Let me talk about the R&D-Styling department. No employee other than styling and of course Mr CTO Mr OWNER is allowed to get in. All though people in other departments works more than styling, but perception of styling people is different. Never saw this idiotic practice in any other design-studios

5. Very important. If anything isn't going right and if you want to share that with Mr OWNER, never do that. He will forward your write-up to the same guy you are complaining against and would tell him to set it right. Next you can make out, what can happen.
